channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over conventional low-load exercise (LLE) and is comparable to high-load exercise (HLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found good agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared with senior physicians in the emergency department for musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A study with 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults found that custom peak-detection algorithms, especially with ankle or hip-worn ActiGraph accelerometers, provided superior accuracy for step count estimation compared to manufacturer's algorithms in post-stroke individuals, particularly at lower walking speeds.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) grouped into three domains (functioning, general well-being, and social participation) and corresponding valid PROMs has been developed for pediatric physical therapy through focus groups, interviews, a scoping review, and expert panel consensus.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: This editorial provides a follow-up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, examining recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of studies up to December 2024 estimated the pooled prevalence of disordered eating (DE) in athletes at 29% (95% CI: 0.25–0.33) and eating disorders (EDs) at 6% (95% CI: 0.04–0.08). Female athletes and those in aesthetic/endurance sports showed higher prevalence.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis (n=62,217 Medicare beneficiaries) found that the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) bundled payment model impacted the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA/mild stroke patients found a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved 6-min walk test distance and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cross-Education Effect on Force Production Capacity After Unilateral Resistance Training in Clinical Populations: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis found the cross-education effect after unilateral resistance training (CE-URT) on force production capacity is present in clinical populations, including those with musculoskeletal conditions and hemiparesis after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-Effectiveness of Post-Acute Stroke Rehabilitation From a U.S. Healthcare Payer Perspective: Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities Versus Skilled Nursing Facilities.
item.description: A decision analytic model evaluating 90-day outcomes for stroke survivors found Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities (IRFs) to be more cost-effective than Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs) from a US healthcare payer perspective.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology found that the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) has clear and certain measurement properties for individuals with stroke, supporting its continued use in rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that the demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting challenges in workforce, access, and awareness.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres
item.description: The UK has launched a pioneering program to test the ARC-EX system, the first dedicated treatment for paralysis approved by UK regulators, which combines targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises to restore strength and movement in spinal cord injury patients.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review identified gaps in rehabilitation strategies for chronic stroke, highlighting a lack of direct comparisons and detailed rehabilitation protocols between contralateral C7 nerve transfer and technology-assisted/high-dose rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicentre retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients (316 ischemic, 249 hemorrhagic) found that admission Berg Balance Scale (BBS) scores independently predicted independent walking at discharge. Optimal cut-off values were derived: 13.5 for ischemic stroke (AUC=0.858) and 12.5 for hemorrhagic stroke (AUC=0.871).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process involving 83 participants from various tennis governing bodies and the broader ecosystem generated recommendations for mental healthcare and wellness promotion among elite tennis athletes, including juniors.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective cohort study at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games monitored 4450 athletes over 14 days, reporting 535 injuries (prevalence=10.9%) and an incidence of 8.6 injuries per 1000 athlete days. Taekwondo (27.3) and football 5-a-side (22.3) had the highest injury incidences.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT (n=30) found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device, Hand Stretcher®, significantly improved flexor spasticity and hand function in patients with chronic stroke after 4 weeks of use.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review identified and analyzed weighted criteria for return-to-play after hamstring injury, highlighting the lack of clear consensus and the high recurrence rate of such injuries.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A pragmatic quasi-experimental study of 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia found a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes.
item.author: PubMed
